questions.body: The pH of the 10^-8 M HCl solution is
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
[H+] = 10^-4 M (from acid)
[H+] = 10^-7 M (from water, as the solution is very dilute)
Total [H+] = 10^-4 + 10^-7 M = 11 × 10^-5 M
pH = -log10 [H+]
= - [log10 11 + log10 10^-5]
= - [1.02 - 5]
= - [-6.98] = + 6.98
Thus, pH of the solution will be greater than 6 but less than 7.

questions.body: Two parallel chords 96 cm and 28 cm long are on the opposite side of the centre of the circle with radius 50 cm. Find the area of the quadrilateral whose vertices are the end points of the chords.
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
AB || CD
x = √50² - 48² = 14
y = √50² - 14² = 48
∴ ABCD is a trapezium
∴ Area of ABCD
= 1/2(96 + 28) × (x + y)
= 1/2(127) 62
= 3844 cm²

questions.body: Select any three distinct digits. Form a three digit number. Form the another number by reversing the digits. Find the difference of these two numbers. What is the G.C.D of all such differences?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
N = abc = 100a + 10b + c
N' = cba = 100 c + 10b + a
N - N' = 99a - 99 c
= 99 (a-c)
So, all such numbers are divisible by 99.
So GCD of all such numbers is 99.

questions.body:
An acid-base titration is a technique where a solution of known concentration of acid/base is used to determine the concentration of an unknown solution of acid/base. These titrations typically use a pH indicator solution to denote the end point of the reaction. A pH indicator is a compound added in small quantities to a solution to indicate the pH visually (generally byappearance/disappearance or change in colour). A typical procedure is as follows:

A certain volume 'V1' of unknown concentration 'M1' of HCl is taken in a conical flask, to which a few drop of phenolphthalein indicator solution is added. The solution remains colourless. From a burette (a graduated dropper) a solution of NaOH, whose concentration is known, 'M2', is added dropwise into the conical flask until a pale pink colour is obtained and is termed as the end point. The amount of solution dispensed from the burette to obtain the end point is noted as 'V2'. Phenolphthalein indicator changes its colour to pink only when the pH of the solution is above 9.5. Similarly, another indicator, methyl orange, is red in colour below pH 3.7 and yellow above. Given below is a graph of pH of the solution in the conical flask and the reading of the burette in the course of the titration. The equivalent point is theoretically defined as the point at which where the number of moles HCl in the conical flask becomes equal to the number of moles of NaOH run down the burette. Note the difference between end point and equivalent point.

Which is the correct graph that represents the titration of NH4OH (from burette) with HCl?
questions.subject: chemistry

questions.body: What is the smallest natural number when multiplied by 15 and divided by 63 gives remainder 21?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let the smallest Natural number = x
Let Quotient = 0
Remainder = 21
ATQ Dividend = Divisor × Quotient + Remainder
x × 15 = 63 × Q + 21
x = (63×Q+21)/15
x = (21×Q+7)/5
if Q = 3
x = (21×3+7)/5 = (63+7)/5
x = 70/5 = 14

questions.body: A lift is moving up at constant speed. Consider the following statements: I. The tension in the string is constant II. The K.E of the elevator is constant III. The gravitational P.E of the earth-lift system is constant IV. The acceleration of the elevator is zero. V. The mechanical energy of the earth-lift system is constant. Choose the correct option

choices.a: Only II and V are true
choices.b: Only IV and V are true
choices.c: Only I, II and III are true
choices.d: Only I, II and IV are true

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
(I) Lift is moving with constant speed in upward direction
so, T - mg = 0 or T = mg constant. (correct)
(II) Kinetic energy = 1/2 mv²
= constant (correct)
(III) a = GMM/r² (not constant) (wrong)
(IV) Velocity is constant so acceleration of lift is zero
(V) P.E. is changing and K.E. is constant so mechanical energy will not be constant (wrong)

questions.body: In the adjoining figure segment AD, BE and CF are the altitudes of triangle ABC. Find AD × BC if AB × AC = 409.6, BE × CF = 202.5.

questions.skip_reason: Cannot solve without the triangle figure showing altitudes
questions.solution:
ar ΔABC = 1/4AB × CP .....(1)
ar ΔABC = 1/4BC × AD .....(2)
ar ΔABC = 1/4AC × BE .....(3)
(1) × (3) (arΔABC)²
= 1/4AB×CP×AC×BE
1/4(AB×AC) (CP×BE)
=1/4(409.6) (202.5) = 20736
ar ΔABC = 144
(1) × (2) × (3) we got
(arΔABC)³
= 1/8 ×AB×CP×BC×AD×AC×BE
(144)³

questions.body: How many four digit numbers divisible by twenty nine have the sum of their digits 29?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
4 digit number = abcd
= 1000 a + 100b + 10c + d
= (986a) + 14a + (87b) + 13b + 10c + d
no is divisible by 29
14a + 13b + 10c + d = 29
n is the remaining number
a + b + c + d = 29
(13a + 12b + 9c) + (a + b + c + d) = 29n
13a + 12b + 9c
= 29m

questions.body: Raju Sharma, a 10th standard student participated in 100 meter sprint. During running he developed painful muscle contraction and fell down. The physical education teacher rushed to him and gave a hot water massage. Raju Sharma slowly recovered from the cramp. The teacher explained the physiology behind the cramp and the subsequent relief. Identify the right explanation.

choices.a: Because of the quick movement, the muscles loose its elasticity and are stressed. The inflammation developed during this process causes cramp. After hot water massage the inflammation subsides and the pain gets relieved.
choices.b: During vigorous physical activity, aerobic respiration in the muscles increases which leads to the accumulation of more CO2 in the muscles. This causes cramps. Later CO2 was relieved upon hot water massage resulting in pain relief.
choices.c: During vigorous physical activity, lactic acid accumulates in the muscles due to anaerobic respiration. This causes the cramps. Hot water massage improves the circulation of blood and O2 in the muscles. As a result lactic acid is converted into CO2 and water. Thus the pain gets relieved.
choices.d: During quick movements, the nerves will not co-operate with the muscles. There is a stimulus which is taken to spinal cord and the effector function was done by motor neurons which cause cramp. On hot water massage the stimulus was subsided. Thus the pain gets relieved.

64 – 48 – * = 0 or divisible by 11
64 – * = 0 or divisible by 11
∴* = 5

questions.body: Sodium reacts with excess oxygen to form sodium oxide. A student wants to prepare 1.24g of sodium oxide. While doing the calculations, he uses atomic number of sodium instead of atomic mass. What is the approximate percentage error in the mass of sodium oxide obtained due to this mistake?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
4Na + O2 → 2Na2O
92 g     124 g
student took mass of Na as 11
∴44g      76 g
To prepare 1.25 g → 76g Na2O
= 44 g Na req.
∴ 1.24 g = (1.25×44)/76 g Na
Na2O = 0.7179 g Na
So he took 0.7179 g Na
But in real →
92 g Na give 124 g Na2O
∴ 0.7179g Na will give
= (0.7179×124)/92 g Na2O
= 0.9676 g Na2O
∴ error in mass of Na2O
= 1.24 – 0.9676 = 0.2724 g
% error = (0.2724×100)/1.24 = 21.97 %
= 22 %

A2+(g) → A3+(g) + e-  3rd Ionization Energy

i. Na+ω is more stable than Naμω.
ii. Na+ω is less stable than Naμω.
iii. Na+ωω is more stable than Naμωω.
iv. Na+ωω is less stable than Naμωω.

questions.body: Light travels from medium X to medium Y as shown in the adjacent figure.

choices.a: both the speed and frequency decrease
choices.c: the speed decreases and wavelength decrease
choices.d: the speed decreases and wavelength increases

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Medium y is denser, so speed will decrease, frequency depends on source so λ will decrease by relation V = nλ

questions.body:
Hess' Law states that 'the heat evolved or absorbed in a chemical reaction is the same whether the process takes place in one or in several steps'
6C(s) + 3H2(g) → C6H6(l) ΔH = heat evolved/absorbed (kJ/mol)
Heat evolved in the combustion of one mole C(s) in excess oxygen is x kJ/mol, and that for the combustion of H2(g) is y kJ/mol and for that of C6H6 (l) is z kJ/mol. Therefore, the heat change (kJ/mol) involved in the formation one mole of C6H6(l) from the given equation is
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: 6C10 + 3H2(g) → C6H60 Now combustion of C10 6C10 + 6O2 → 6CO2; ΔH1 = 6x..(i) 3H2 + 3/2 O2 → 3H2O (l) ; ΔH2 = 3x....(ii) C6H6 + 15/2 O2 → 3H2O ; 6CO2 ; ΔH = Z Reverse the reaction. 6CO2 + 3H2O → C6H6 + 15/2 O2 ΔH3 = -Z.....(iii) After adding equation (i), (ii), (iii) 6C10 + 3H2(g) → C6H6(l) ; ΔH ΔH = ΔH1 + ΔH2 + ΔH3 = 6x + 3y - z

questions.body: If A(p, q + r), B(q, r + p) and C(r, p + q) are points then area of triangle ABC
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: A(p + q + r) ; B(q, r + p) ; C(r,p+q) Area of (ΔABC) = 1/2[p(r + p - p - q) +q(p + q - q - r) + r(q + r - r - p)] = 1/2[pr + pq - pq - qr - qr - pr] = 0

questions.body: A particle moves along the x-axis according to the equation x = 6t² where x is displacement in meters and t is time in seconds. Therefore

choices.a: the acceleration of the particle is 6 ms⁻²
choices.b: the particle follows a parabolic path
choices.c: each second the velocity of the particle changes by 9.8 ms⁻¹
choices.d: the velocity of the particle is 6 ms⁻¹ at t = 0.5 s

questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: x = 6t^2 dx/dt = V = 12t at t = 0 initial velocity u = 0 d^2x/dt^2 = a = 12, t = 0.5 sec. V = u + at V = 0 + (12) (0.5 = 6 m/s)

questions.body: What occurs when H₂O(l) evaporates (i) Covalent bonds are broken. (ii) Only dipole-dipole forces are overcome. (iii)Heat is absorbed by water from the surroundings. (iv) It becomes oxygen and hydrogen gas.
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: In evaporation, water takes heat, overcomes the intermolecular force of attraction and concerts into vapour phase.
